meta.title: Tag group
meta.description: A tag group is a compact, interactive element. It categorizes, labels, or adds attributes to objects for quick recognition and filtering.

toc.value: Toggle buttons vs clearable tag groups

mdx:
## Overview

The tag group organizes a collection of individual tag components, allowing
users to efficiently scan and understand multiple pieces of metadata or filter
criteria associated with a product, order, or entity in a compact, manageable
space.

### Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

[Figma library](https://www.figma.com/design/AvtPX6g7OGGCRvNlatGOIY/NIMBUS-design-system?node-id=4292-25466)
[Storybook](https://nimbus-storybook.vercel.app/?path=/docs/components-taggroup--docs)

## Variables

Get familiar with the features.

### Visual options

#### Size

The large size of the tag within tag groups is the default, the small size is
meant for more compact uses. The more tags present, consider smaller sizes to
make the information more compact and easily read for the user.

```jsx live
const App = () => {
  const smallTag = [{ id: "cherries", name: "Cherries" }];
  const mediumTag = [{ id: "kiwis", name: "Kiwis" }];
  const largeTag = [{ id: "apples", name: "Apples" }];

  return (
    <Stack direction="row" gap="400" alignItems="flex-start">
      <Box>
        <Text as="p" textStyle="sm" fontWeight="bold" marginBottom="200">Large</Text>
        <TagGroup.Root size="lg" aria-label="Large tags" onRemove={() => {}}>
          <TagGroup.TagList items={largeTag}>
            {(item) => <TagGroup.Tag>{item.name}</TagGroup.Tag>}
          </TagGroup.TagList>
        </TagGroup.Root>
      </Box>
      <Box>
        <Text as="p" textStyle="sm" fontWeight="bold" marginBottom="200">Medium</Text>
        <TagGroup.Root size="md" aria-label="Medium tags" onRemove={() => {}}>
          <TagGroup.TagList items={mediumTag}>
            {(item) => <TagGroup.Tag>{item.name}</TagGroup.Tag>}
          </TagGroup.TagList>
        </TagGroup.Root>
      </Box>
      <Box>
        <Text as="p" textStyle="sm" fontWeight="bold" marginBottom="200">Small</Text>
        <TagGroup.Root size="sm" aria-label="Small tags" onRemove={() => {}}>
          <TagGroup.TagList items={smallTag}>
            {(item) => <TagGroup.Tag>{item.name}</TagGroup.Tag>}
          </TagGroup.TagList>
        </TagGroup.Root>
      </Box>
    </Stack>
  );
}
```

### Toggle buttons vs clearable tag groups

Allow users to toggle the active state of a tag, or be able to remove tags by
adding a "close" icon to each tag. Avoid mixing these interactions within the
same group.

```jsx live
const App = () => {
  const [selectedToggleTags, setSelectedToggleTags] = useState(new Set(["vegetables"]));

  const removableTags = [
    { id: "remove1", name: "Remove me first" },
    { id: "remove2", name: "Remove me second" },
    { id: "remove3", name: "Remove me last" },
  ];

  const toggleTags = [
    { id: "fruits", name: "Fruits" },
    { id: "vegetables", name: "Vegetables" },
    { id: "meat", name: "Meat" },
  ];

  return (
    <Stack direction="column" gap="600" alignItems="flex-start">
      <Box>
        <Text as="p" textStyle="sm" marginBottom="200" fontWeight="bold">Removable tag group</Text>
        <TagGroup.Root aria-label="Removable tags" onRemove={() => {}}>
          <TagGroup.TagList items={removableTags}>
            {(item) => <TagGroup.Tag>{item.name}</TagGroup.Tag>}
          </TagGroup.TagList>
        </TagGroup.Root>
      </Box>

      <Box>
        <Text as="p" textStyle="sm" marginBottom="200" fontWeight="bold">Toggleable tag group</Text>
        <TagGroup.Root
          aria-label="Toggle tags"
          selectionMode="multiple"
          selectedKeys={selectedToggleTags}
          onSelectionChange={setSelectedToggleTags}
        >
          <TagGroup.TagList items={toggleTags}>
            {(item) => <TagGroup.Tag>{item.name}</TagGroup.Tag>}
          </TagGroup.TagList>
        </TagGroup.Root>
      </Box>
    </Stack>
  );
}
```

```jsx live
const App = () => {
```

a11y.mdx:
## Accessibility

Accessibility ensures that digital content and functionality are usable by
everyone, including people with disabilities, by addressing visual, auditory,
cognitive, and physical limitations.

```jsx live
const App = () => {
  const vegetableOptions = [
  { id: "tomato", name: "Tomato" },
  { id: "zucchini", name: "Zucchini" },
  { id: "peppers", name: "Peppers" },
  ];

  return (
    <TagGroup.Root onRemove={() => {}}>
      <TagGroup.TagList items={vegetableOptions}>
        {(item) => <TagGroup.Tag>{item.name}</TagGroup.Tag>}
      </TagGroup.TagList>
    </TagGroup.Root>
  );
}
```

### Accessibility standards

- **Keyboard focus:** Ensure the user can navigate to and between individual
  tags (if interactive/removable) using the Tab key.
- **ARIA labels for removal:** For removable tags, the close button/icon must
  have a clear `aria-label` (e.g., "Remove filter: [Tag Label]") to inform
  screen readers exactly what is being dismissed.
- **Contrast:** Verify that the text contrast ratio against the tag's background
  color meets WCAG AA standards (4.5:1).
- **Logical reading order:** The order in which the tags are read by a screen
  reader must logically align with the visual order.

guidelines.mdx:
## Guidelines

These guidelines provide comprehensive direction for designing and developing
tags within Nimbus.

### Best practices

- **Limit the number of tags:** Avoid overwhelming users with too many tags.
- **Maintain consistent styling:** Ensure tags look and behave uniformly across
  the product.
- **Provide clear affordance for removal:** If tags are removable, make the
  close icon visible and interactive.
- **Do not mix toggle-able and removable tags within the same area:** These are
  different types of selective interactions, avoid mixing to avoid confusion and
  clear use in your interface.
- **Single context:** Ensure all tags within a single group are relevant to one
  entity or topic (e.g., all tags describe a single product's features, or all
  tags represent filters applied to a list).
- **Conciseness:** Keep tag labels short and easily readable (ideally one or two
  words). If the text is too long, it should be truncated, and a tooltip should
  reveal the full context.
- **Logical ordering:** Arrange tags in a predictable, logical sequence, such as
  alphabetically, by date added, or by importance.
- **Color consistency:** Use color consistently to convey meaning. Define a
  clear palette for status tags (e.g., Green for "In Stock") and neutral colors
  for simple categorization.
- **Handling overflow:** Implement a clear strategy for managing too many tags
  (e.g., collapsing the list and showing a "+X more" count or allowing
  horizontal scrolling).

### Usage

Tags are used to display keywords. They also help filter data, allowing users to
narrow down lists of items, and show selected options, representing choices in
multi-select inputs.

> [!TIP]\
> When to use

- **Displaying metadata/attributes:** To showcase several non-hierarchical
  attributes of an item (e.g., product size, material, color, collection name).
- **Filtering statuses (interactive tags):** To show the user which filters are
  currently applied, often allowing them to click an 'x' to remove the filter
  immediately.
- **Categorization (non-interactive tags):** To quickly label and organize
  content pieces (e.g., "New," "Beta," "Requires Review").
- **Highlighting key status:** To draw attention to critical, secondary status
  information (e.g., "Out of Stock," "Draft").

> [!CAUTION]\
> When not to use

- **Primary navigation:** Do not use tags to replace main menu links or primary
  navigation structures.
- **Long sentences/descriptions:** If you need to convey complex sentences or
  lengthy descriptions, use standard body text or a detail panel instead.
- **Too many options:** If the list of possible tags is extremely long and
  variable (over 10-15 per group), consider using a multi-select dropdown with
  categorization instead.
- **Destructive actions:** Tags should not be used as the sole trigger for
  complex, destructive, or irreversible actions.
- **Mixing toggle-able and removable tags:** is not a good use of tooling, it
  creates a very confusing experience for users.

### Tags and tag group in use

> [!TIP]\
> **Do**
>
> - Do keep tag phrases short and descriptive.
> - Do keep removable tags easy to remove, and toggle-able tags easy to click.
>   Avoid moving them or shuffling them while a user is interacting with them.
>   Moving targets are harder to click.
> - Do keep styling and sizing consistent within the same context.
> - Do limit the amount of tags per context to not overwhelm the UI.

```jsx live
const App = () => {
  const [selected, setSelected] = useState(
    new Set(["pop", "jazz", "hip-hop", "reggae"])
  );

  const musicGenres = [
    { id: "rock", name: "Rock" },
    { id: "pop", name: "Pop" },
    { id: "jazz", name: "Jazz" },
    { id: "hip-hop", name: "Hip Hop" },
    { id: "classical", name: "Classical" },
    { id: "reggae", name: "Reggae" },
    { id: "electronic", name: "Electronic" },
    { id: "country", name: "Country" },
  ];

  return (
    <Box width="9600">
      <TagGroup.Root
        aria-label="Music genres"
        selectionMode="multiple"
        selectedKeys={selected}
        onSelectionChange={setSelected}
      >
        <TagGroup.TagList items={musicGenres} gap="300">
          {(item) => <TagGroup.Tag>{item.name}</TagGroup.Tag>}
        </TagGroup.TagList>
      </TagGroup.Root>
    </Box>
  );
};
```

> [!CAUTION]\
> **Don't**
>
> - Don’t use long, multi-word phrases for tags.
> - Don't mix clearable or toggle status within tag groups. All should have the
>   same functions within the same context.
> - Don't overload the UI with an excessive number of tags.
> - Don't use colors that are a misuse of semantic meanings. Use the neutral
>   color options if colors are meant to show variety.

```jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack gap="300">
    <Text fontWeight="700">Select music categories</Text>
    <Flex gap="300" flexWrap="wrap">
      <TagGroup.Root aria-label="non-removable music tags" selectionMode="none">
        <TagGroup.TagList gap="300">
          <TagGroup.Tag id="pop" colorPalette="warning">
            Pop
          </TagGroup.Tag>
          <TagGroup.Tag id="jazz" colorPalette="positive">
            Jazz
          </TagGroup.Tag>
          <TagGroup.Tag id="reggae" colorPalette="warning">
            Reggae
          </TagGroup.Tag>
          <TagGroup.Tag id="electronic" colorPalette="critical">
            Electronic
          </TagGroup.Tag>
          <TagGroup.Tag id="hip-hop" colorPalette="positive">
            Hip Hop
          </TagGroup.Tag>
        </TagGroup.TagList>
      </TagGroup.Root>
      <TagGroup.Root
        aria-label="removable music tags"
        selectionMode="none"
        onRemove={() => {}}
      >
        <TagGroup.TagList gap="300">
          <TagGroup.Tag id="rock" colorPalette="critical">
            Rock
          </TagGroup.Tag>
          <TagGroup.Tag id="classical" colorPalette="warning">
            Classical
          </TagGroup.Tag>
          <TagGroup.Tag id="country" colorPalette="critical">
            Country
          </TagGroup.Tag>
        </TagGroup.TagList>
      </TagGroup.Root>
    </Flex>
  </Stack>
);
```
